texto: It is a crushing moment when you realize that your life has either been a series of huge mistakes, or worse; it hasn’t.

frase_original: It is a crushing moment when you realize that your life has either been a series of huge mistakes, or worse; it hasn’t.

texto: I’d like to think that the day I realize we will always be miserable will differ from every other. I hope it will so obnoxiously stand out from the monotonous cycle of my days, that I wouldn’t forget that sorrowful moment of comprehension. But, when that breeze of reality comes by, it wont be a memorable hurricane, nor a momentous tornado. It will be the same, sad, soft wind that I felt the day before, and the day before that. Because the moment you understand your inevitable misery in life, may also be the day you see you are to always be dejected.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Temperance: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “I’d like to think that the day I realize we will always be miserable will differ from every other…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, depressed, depressing pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de depressing e vida.

frase_original: I’d like to think that the day I realize we will always be miserable will differ from every other. I hope it will so obnoxiously stand out from the monotonous cycle of my days, that I wouldn’t forget that sorrowful moment of comprehension. But, when that breeze of reality comes by, it wont be a memorable hurricane, nor a momentous tornado. It will be the same, sad, soft wind that I felt the day before, and the day before that. Because the moment you understand your inevitable misery in life, may also be the day you see you are to always be dejected.
